### Post by peterw555 on 2005-03-30
Having posted a log yesterday I had a reply but unfortunately it didnt work. My problem is after installin Red Hat Linux Workstation 10 on a new Dell Inspiron 9200 it doesn seem to want to install the ATI Radeon graphics card for 3d use.... The XORG.Conf file I was told to copy in to \etc\X11 is below

# XF86Config-4 (XFree86 X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.

# md5sum /etc/X11/XF86Config-4 >/var/lib/xfree86/XF86Config-4.md5sum
# dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xfree86

Section "Module"
Load "GLcore"
Load "bitmap"
Load "dbe"
Load "ddc"
Load "dri"
Load "extmod"
Load "freetype"
Load "glx"
Load "int10"

Identifier "Generic Keyboard"
Driver "keyboard"
Option "CoreKeyboard"
Option "XkbRules" "xfree86"
Option "XkbModel" "pc104"
Option "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Configured Mouse"

Option "CorePointer"
Option "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
Option "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
Option "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Identifier "Synaptics Touchpad"
Driver "synaptics"
Option "SendCoreEvents" "true"
Option "Device" "/dev/psaux"
Option "Protocol" "auto-dev"
EndSection

Section "Device"
Identifier "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility 9600/9700 M10/M11 (RV350 NP)"
Driver "fglrx"
BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
VideoRam 65536
#Option "UseFBDev" "true"
Option "EnablePageFlip" "true"
Option "AGPMode" "4"
Option "AGPFastWrite" "on"
Option "UseInternalAGPGART" "yes"
Option "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
Option "NoLogo" "true"
#Option "RenderAccel" "true"
Option "backingstore" "true"
Option "UseFastTLS" "0"
Option "BlockSignalsOnLock" "on"
Option "ForceGenericCPU" "no"
Option "VideoOverlay" "on"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
Identifier "Generic Monitor"
HorizSync 30-90
VertRefresh 50-75
Option "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Default Screen"
Device "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility 9600/9700 M10/M11 (RV350 NP)"
Monitor "Generic Monitor"
DefaultDepth 24

Depth 1
Modes "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"

Modes "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"

### Post by Locomorto on 2005-03-30
First, next time encase your xorg.conf in code (```

```) tags, its much easier to read that way ;).

Second, are you using ubuntu or not? If your not your better of going to the Novell Forums, or emailing them (assuming you can).

### Post by songochain on 2005-03-31
Have u installed ati drivers? If not, change fgrlx to ati or radeon
